finding: Correct. import.meta.env.DEV is a Vite compile-time constant → prod becomes `false ? [ {import('@/dev/gallery/GalleryPage')...} ] : []`; the true-branch (holding the only dynamic import to GalleryPage) is unreachable and Rollup DCE-drops it, so GalleryPage + registry.ts + all mock cassettes are tree-shaken out. No residual top-level reference. else-branch is []. Prior top-level `const GalleryPage = lazyWithPreload(import(...))` kept the import live — genuinely fixed.

angle: prod-exclusion runtime marker survives minify + NOT stripped
finding: Correct. Marker is `data-gallery-build-marker="ZIEE_GALLERY_SEED_MARKER"` — a used JSX string-literal attribute value, so it survives minification if the module ships. vite-plugin-remove-data-test.js strips ONLY /data-test[a-zA-Z0-9-_]*/ attributes; `data-gallery-build-marker` starts with data-gallery, NOT data-test, so it is NOT stripped. The comment's claim is accurate. Marker string occurs only in gallery files (one runtime value in GalleryPage.tsx; two in registry.ts comments which prod strips) → no false-fail source from app-main code.

Correct. DIST=src-app/dist/ui matches vite root:'src' + outDir:'../../dist/ui'; that outDir is outside vite root so vite refuses to emptyOutDir → stale chunks persist. fs.rmSync(DIST,{recursive,force}) before build:nocheck removes them. Only runs under --build; deletes regenerated build output only. Grep walks .js/.mjs/.cjs for the marker string.

Correct + imported in both new gate scripts (gen-gallery-seed-registry.mjs:21 and gen-overlay-registry.mjs:34 both import pathToFileURL; both use `import.meta.url === pathToFileURL(process.argv[1]).href`). Portable on Windows/spaced paths.

Not introduced by this commit and out of its stated scope ('both new gate scripts'), but noting: gen-override-registry.mjs:188 and seam-codemod.mjs:202 still use the naive `file://${process.argv[1]}` form and would be silently disabled on Windows. Pre-existing; no regression from this commit.

Correct. [data-gallery-frame] exists on ALL page-class renderers in both workspaces (ui pages.tsx:168, deepStates.tsx:60, seededSurfaces.tsx:58; desktop pages.tsx:158 added here). Single-surface `only=` mode renders exactly one section → exactly one data-gallery-frame → no Playwright strict-mode multi-match. Threshold 40→20 measures rendered content not chrome; strictly better at catching empty seeds (chrome-only ~50 chars previously false-passed >40).

angle: e2e overlays use a different assertion (no hang risk)
finding: Correct. Overlay tests (which portal role=dialog to body and whose OverlayFrame has NO data-gallery-frame) do NOT use the frame-subtree helper — they assert getByRole('dialog').first().toBeVisible(). So the data-gallery-frame assertion is never applied to overlays; no hang.

Valid. js-tool registers route /settings/js-tool → slug settings-js-tool; its data comes only from web js-tool/gallery.tsx cassette key JsTool.getSettings, inherited into the desktop gallery via module-seed.ts MODULE_CASSETTE (SHARED_CASSETTE spread). Desktop gallery seed.ts calls loadModules() (web core, applies CORE_MODULE_BLOCKLIST) + loadDesktopModules(); js-tool is NOT blocklisted (only server-update/user-profile), so the page resolves. Page renders a Card + many labeled InputNumbers → well over 20 chars. Genuinely proves cross-workspace inheritance (settings-users was crawl-covered and would pass even if inheritance broke).

Safe. authCassette (fixtures/auth.ts:41) already seeds Auth.getSessionSettings with a valid SessionSettings shape (access_token_expiry_hours:24, refresh_token_expiry_days:30, updated_at). Removed inline override had identical values except a cosmetic updated_at; no key dropped. /settings/sessions still hydrates → gallery-gap-seed 'settings-sessions' test still green.

Safe. Only importer of module-seed.ts is fixtures/index.ts, which imports MODULE_CASSETTE only — never OVERLAY_ENTRIES. Desktop overlays come from overlays.tsx's own `OVERLAY_ENTRIES = []`; the removed export was dead (never wired) so no runtime/behavior change. Removed OverlayEntry type + SHARED_OVERLAYS imports were used only by the deleted export → no unused-import lint error.

No regression. New /surface:\s*['"]([^'"]+)['"]/g is a strict superset of the old single-quote form — still matches single quotes, additionally matches double. TEST-5 unit tests pass. Adding matches can only make the overlay gate MORE complete (catch more wired surfaces), never mask an un-rendered one.

No false-reject. New /...\[approved:[^\]]*\]/ requires a CLOSED token; a well-formed `- NO-SEED: mod — reason [approved: who date]` still matches (TEST-4 'well-formed parsed' + 'missing sign-off rejected' both green). Real GALLERY_SEED_EXCEPTIONS.md has zero actual entries (only a backtick-wrapped template line that ^- correctly does not match). Stricter than before; no valid line lost.

Harmless. No gallery.ts files exist in the repo, so the added `e !== 'gallery.ts'` clauses (gen-gallery-coverage x2, gen-state-matrix) are currently no-ops (future-proofing). In gen-gallery-coverage the clause is additionally dead because the same predicate already requires /\.tsx$/ (gallery.ts can never reach it). Not a defect — no behavior change and cannot exclude a real surface.
